sourceMovie.title: Hedda
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Hedda works as a drama movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The drama and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Romeo + Juliet
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Hedda clicked for you, Romeo + Juliet (1996) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and romance, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Talented Mr. Ripley
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Hedda clicked for you, The Talented Mr. Ripley (1999) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Hedda?
faq.answer: Try Romeo + Juliet, The Talented Mr. Ripley, Mississippi Burning—each shares DNA with Hedda while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Hedda?
faq.answer: Hedda (2025) is best described as Drama, Thriller, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Hedda worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 5.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
